nodes.name: dataset_state_initialization
nodes.description: Build an immutable dataset state (sequence_labels, sequence_strs) either from direct in-memory inputs or by loading/parsing a FASTA file.

inputs.name: sequence_labels
inputs.constraints: Required when constructing directly; length must match sequence_strs.

inputs.name: sequence_strs
inputs.constraints: Required when constructing directly; one sequence per label.

inputs.name: fasta_file
inputs.constraints: Optional alternate constructor input; if provided, sequences are parsed from file.

outputs.name: dataset_state
outputs.constraints: Immutable logical state passed to downstream atoms.

nodes.type_signature: (sequence_labels: List[str], sequence_strs: List[str], fasta_file: str | Path) -> FastaDatasetState{sequence_labels: List[str], sequence_strs: List[str]}
nodes.conceptual_summary: Canonical Immutable Paired-Sequence State Constructor. Constructs a canonical immutable state object containing two index-aligned lists: identifier tokens and associated symbol sequences. The constructor supports two ingestion paths that converge to the same internal representation: direct in-memory arrays or parsing from a structured text file into equivalent arrays. It enforces structural validity constraints (especially one-to-one cardinality alignment) before freezing the state for downstream computation.. Applications: Initializing an immutable corpus state of document IDs and token strings from either RAM buffers or line-delimited text archives in natural language processing, Building a fixed experiment state of trial IDs and temporal signal traces from direct arrays or instrument export files in biomedical signal analysis, Creating a read-only simulation input state of entity IDs and event-sequence encodings from memory or configuration files in discrete-event systems engineering

nodes.name: normalize_direct_sequence_inputs
nodes.description: Create immutable list snapshots of in-memory sequence labels and sequence strings for the direct-constructor path.
nodes.type_signature: (sequence_labels: List[str] | Iterable[str], sequence_strs: List[str] | Iterable[str]) -> tuple[List[str], List[str]]

nodes.name: parse_fasta_records_with_header_fallback
nodes.description: Scan FASTA lines, flush completed records on new headers, concatenate sequence buffers, and synthesize a fallback label `seqnum{line_idx:09d}` when a header is empty.

inputs.name: fasta_file
inputs.constraints: Readable FASTA-like text file; optional when using direct inputs

outputs.name: file_labels
outputs.constraints: One label per parsed sequence record

outputs.name: file_strs
outputs.constraints: Each entry is concatenated sequence lines for corresponding label

nodes.name: select_sequence_source
nodes.description: Choose canonical sequence payload from either parsed FASTA records or normalized in-memory lists.

inputs.name: direct_labels
inputs.constraints: Used when no FASTA source is selected

inputs.name: direct_strs
inputs.constraints: Used when no FASTA source is selected

inputs.name: file_labels
inputs.constraints: Used when FASTA source is selected

inputs.name: file_strs
inputs.constraints: Used when FASTA source is selected

inputs.name: fasta_file
inputs.constraints: Presence indicates FASTA path is active

outputs.name: selected_strs
outputs.constraints: Canonical sequence strings for dataset state

nodes.type_signature: (direct_labels: List[str], direct_strs: List[str], file_labels: List[str], file_strs: List[str], fasta_file: str | Path) -> tuple[List[str], List[str]]

nodes.name: validate_label_uniqueness
nodes.description: Enforce that sequence labels are unique by comparing list size with set cardinality.

inputs.name: selected_labels
inputs.constraints: Must be finite; duplicates are invalid

nodes.name: assemble_dataset_state
nodes.description: Construct the immutable dataset state object from validated labels and aligned sequence strings.

inputs.name: selected_strs
inputs.constraints: Same length and order as labels

outputs.name: dataset_state
outputs.constraints: Immutable logical state for downstream use

nodes.type_signature: (validated_labels: List[str], selected_strs: List[str]) -> FastaDatasetState{sequence_labels: List[str], sequence_strs: List[str]}

nodes.name: dataset_length_query
nodes.description: Return the number of labeled sequences in dataset_state.
nodes.conceptual_summary: Labeled Collection Cardinality Extractor. This operation maps a state container to a single scalar by reading the cardinality of a required identifier index within that container. It does not transform, reorder, or inspect element content; it only extracts structural size information from the current state snapshot. The output is an exact non-negative integer equal to the current number of indexed entries.. Applications: Counting annotated waveform segments in a biomedical signal processing registry before batched analysis, Retrieving the number of pending work-unit IDs in a distributed scheduler state for resource allocation, Measuring labeled tile count in a remote-sensing annotation manifest prior to training/evaluation split generation, Querying component-identifier count in a mechanical assembly model to gate downstream simulation complexity

nodes.name: dataset_item_retrieval
nodes.description: Retrieve a single labeled sequence by index from immutable dataset_state.

inputs.name: dataset_state
inputs.constraints: Must contain sequence_labels and sequence_strs with equal length.

inputs.name: idx
inputs.constraints: Index must be valid for dataset length.

nodes.conceptual_summary: aligned_pair_index_lookup. Given an immutable container composed of two positionally aligned 1-D collections, this operation performs direct integer-index selection into both collections at the same coordinate. It returns the paired elements as a 2-tuple, preserving alignment semantics without modifying source state. The transform is a lossless projection from a size-N paired collection to a single pair under a validity constraint on the index.. Applications: Signal processing: retrieve one (channel_id, sampled_trace) pair from aligned channel metadata and waveform arrays., Computer vision: fetch one (annotation_tag, encoded_feature_string) pair from parallel label/descriptor stores., Network engineering: select one (node_identifier, serialized_state_vector) pair from indexed topology snapshots., Manufacturing analytics: access one (part_code, sensor_event_sequence) pair from synchronized production records.

nodes.name: token_budget_batch_planning
nodes.description: Compute batch index groups under a token budget using sequence strings and per-sequence overhead.

outputs.name: batch_indices
outputs.constraints: Each inner list is a batch of dataset indices satisfying token budget.

nodes.type_signature: (dataset_state: FastaDatasetState, toks_per_batch: int, extra_toks_per_seq: int) -> List[List[int]]
nodes.conceptual_summary: Length-Constrained Sequential Packing. Transforms an ordered collection of variable-size elements into nested index groups under a fixed per-group capacity constraint. Each element contributes a cost equal to its intrinsic length plus a constant per-element overhead. A greedy single-pass rule appends the next element if feasible; otherwise it closes the current group and starts a new one.. Applications: Satellite telemetry framing: pack variable-length sensor records into downlink frames with fixed per-record protocol overhead, Additive manufacturing job staging: group parts into print runs under machine-time limits with per-part setup overhead, Medical imaging pipeline orchestration: bundle scans of varying slice counts into compute batches under memory limits with per-scan preprocessing overhead, Robotics communication scheduling: aggregate variable-size control/state messages into cycle windows with fixed per-message encoding overhead
